LGP-MAINT-001.content: Routine inhibitor top-up performed on Tank ST-11.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: Routine inhibitor top-up performed on Tank ST-11.

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Added 120 litres of TBC (Tertiary Butyl Catechol) solution at 50 ppm concentration.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: TBC (Tertiary Butyl Catechol) solution added: 120 litres @ 50 ppm concentration.

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Tank temperature was 16.4°C, which is within the acceptable range of ≤20°C.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: Tank temperature at time of dosing: 16.4°C (within acceptable range ≤20°C).

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Cooling brine circulation flow rate was 18 m³/h.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: Cooling brine circulation confirmed operational; flow rate 18 m³/h.

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Dissolved oxygen level sampled from top tap was 6.2 ppm.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: Dissolved oxygen level sampled from top tap: 6.2 ppm (acceptable).

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Next scheduled inhibitor top-up is set for 2020-04-18 per OEM SOP-ST-003.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: NEXT SCHEDULED TOP-UP: 2020-04-18 (30-day interval per OEM SOP-ST-003).

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Plant shutdown scheduled for 2020-03-24 due to anticipated COVID-19 lockdown order.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: Plant shutdown for scheduled maintenance from 2020-03-24 (COVID-19 lockdown order anticipated).

LGP-MAINT-001.content: Shutdown inhibitor protocol to be followed on 2020-03-23 before facility closure.
LGP-MAINT-001.source_span: Supervisor K. Srinivas verbally confirmed that "shutdown inhibitor protocol" should be followed on 2020-03-23 before gates close.

Transfer pumps P-01 through P-04 were isolated and locked.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: Transfer pumps P-01 through P-04 isolated and locked

LGP-MAINT-002.content: Nitrogen blanket was confirmed on ST-09 and ST-10.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: Nitrogen blanket confirmed on ST-09 and ST-10

LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: ST-11 nitrogen blanket: blanketing valve NV-11 found to be sticking; deferred to next shift.

LGP-MAINT-002.content: Brine chiller compressor CHR-01 was set to reduced-load mode.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: Brine chiller compressor CHR-01: set to reduced-load mode

LGP-MAINT-002.content: Chiller flow confirmation on ST-11 loop could not be performed because the instrument panel is offline for upgrade.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: Chiller flow confirmation on ST-11 loop: instrument panel offline for upgrade (scheduled since Feb 2020).

LGP-MAINT-002.content: TBC inhibitor top-up for extended shutdown was not completed because the inhibitor drum was empty.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: TBC inhibitor top-up for extended shutdown: technician MT-07 states inhibitor drum was empty

LGP-MAINT-002.content: A requisition for inhibitor refill was submitted under reference PUR-2020-0412.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: requisition for refill submitted (Requisition Ref: PUR-2020-0412)

LGP-MAINT-002.content: ST-11 is currently at 78% fill level.
There is a risk of temperature rise in ST-11 if the chiller fails during the shutdown.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: If chiller fails during shutdown, temp will rise.

LGP-MAINT-002.content: The risk regarding ST-11 temperature rise was not escalated to the plant manager's log.
LGP-MAINT-002.source_span: No record of this being escalated to plant manager's log.

LGP-OEM-003.content: For shutdowns exceeding 7 days, a minimum TBC concentration of 15 ppm in the liquid phase is required.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: Prior to any plant shutdown exceeding 7 days, the operator MUST perform an inhibitor top-up to achieve a minimum TBC concentration of 15 ppm in the liquid phase.

LGP-OEM-003.content: For shutdowns exceeding 30 days, the minimum TBC concentration must be 25 ppm.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: For shutdowns exceeding 30 days, the minimum concentration increases to 25 ppm.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Dissolved oxygen must be verified to be at least 5 ppm via grab sample before closing tank inspection ports.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: Dissolved oxygen must be verified ≥5 ppm via grab sample before closing tank inspection ports.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Stagnant tanks without circulation may develop an oxygen gradient, leading to oxygen depletion at the tank bottom within 7–14 days.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: Stagnant tanks with no circulation will develop an oxygen gradient; liquid near the tank bottom may become oxygen-depleted within 7–14 days depending on temperature.

LGP-OEM-003.content: The brine chiller circuit must remain operational if ambient temperatures are forecast to exceed 25°C.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: The brine chiller circuit MUST remain in operation during any shutdown where ambient temperatures are forecast to exceed 25°C.

LGP-OEM-003.content: The acceptable tank temperature during shutdown is 20°C or less.
Chiller maintenance is limited to a maximum of 72 hours before styrene must be transferred to an alternate tank.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: If chiller is taken offline for maintenance, maximum allowable duration is 72 hours; above this, styrene must be transferred to an alternate tank with active cooling.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Auto-polymerisation of styrene is an exothermic reaction that becomes self-accelerating above 30°C.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: Auto-polymerisation of styrene is an exothermic reaction. Once initiated above ~30°C, the reaction is self-accelerating.

LGP-OEM-003.content: TBC inhibitor loses effectiveness at temperatures above 52°C.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: At temperatures above 52°C, TBC inhibitor loses effectiveness.

LGP-OEM-003.content: The heat of polymerisation is approximately 660 kJ/kg and can cause a runaway feedback loop.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: The heat of polymerisation (approximately 660 kJ/kg) will further raise tank temperature in a runaway feedback loop.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Each tank must have at least one calibrated RTD probe installed at mid-level.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: At least one calibrated RTD (resistance temperature detector) probe must be installed at mid-level on each tank.

LGP-OEM-003.content: The warning alarm is set at 18°C.
The critical alarm is set at 25°C.
The emergency shutdown trigger is set at 30°C.
If the alarm system is offline, manual temperature readings must be recorded every 4 hours.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: If the alarm system is offline for any reason, manual temperature readings must be recorded every 4 hours and initialled by a qualified operator.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Before re-commissioning after a shutdown >14 days, sample liquid from top and bottom for TBC and effectiveness testing.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: (a) Sample liquid styrene from top and bottom of tank for TBC concentration and inhibitor effectiveness testing.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Before re-commissioning after a shutdown >14 days, check dissolved oxygen at three depths.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: (b) Check dissolved oxygen at three depths.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Before re-commissioning after a shutdown >14 days, verify chiller brine flow rate before starting transfer pumps.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: (c) Verify chiller brine flow rate before starting transfer pumps.

LGP-OEM-003.content: Do not initiate transfers until temperature is confirmed to be ≤18°C.
LGP-OEM-003.source_span: (d) Do not initiate transfers until temperature ≤18°C is confirmed.

LGP-PTW-004.content: Permit PTW-2020-0189 was issued for work on Brine Chiller Unit CHR-01.
Replacement of chiller compressor drive belt and coolant pump seal inspection was scheduled.

Replacement of chiller compressor drive belt and
                   coolant pump seal inspection.

LGP-PTW-004.content: Styrene tank temperatures must be less than or equal to 18 degrees Celsius before chiller shutdown.
LGP-PTW-004.source_span: Styrene tank temperatures must be ≤18°C before chiller shutdown begins.

LGP-PTW-004.content: Work was completed and the pump seal was replaced with an OEM part.

Work completed: 2020-01-16 11:30
[ ✓] Recommissioning flow test: 18.2 m³/h — PASS
"Pump seal found worn; replaced with OEM part.

Seal inspection was deferred to the next scheduled PM in April 2020.

May need recalibration.

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: Shift Engineer D. Prasad observed the temperature display for Tank ST-11 reading 27.3°C, which is above the acceptable limit of 20°C.
LGP-INCIDENT-019.source_span: At 14:45, during routine tank farm walkthrough, Shift Engineer D. Prasad noticed that the temperature display for Tank ST-11 on the main control panel was reading 27.3°C — above the acceptable limit of 20°C.

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: The temperature of Tank ST-11 was 27.3°C.

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: A chiller system partial flow restriction occurred due to a partially blocked brine strainer.

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: The temperature alarm failed to trigger because the alarm relay contacts were corroded.
LGP-INCIDENT-019.source_span: However: The temperature alarm (setpoint 18°C warning, 25°C critical) had NOT triggered despite the 27.3°C reading. Investigation found the alarm relay contacts corroded.

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: Adding a second independent high-temp alarm on ST-11 was deferred because the site engineer stated one alarm should be sufficient.
LGP-INCIDENT-019.source_span: Recommended: add second independent high-temp alarm on ST-11. [NOT DONE — deferred per site engineer response: "one alarm should be sufficient."]

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: A monthly alarm function test for all tank farm instruments was not implemented because no procedure or schedule was created.
LGP-INCIDENT-019.source_span: Recommended: monthly alarm function test for all tank farm instruments. [NOT DONE — no procedure created, no schedule set.]

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: No entry was made in the risk register because the process engineer decided the low-severity near-miss did not warrant it.
LGP-INCIDENT-019.source_span: RISK REGISTER ENTRY: Not made (incident classified as low-severity near-miss; process engineer decided risk register entry not warranted).

LGP-INCIDENT-019.content: The incident was not cross-referenced to a previous finding regarding a flow sensor anomaly on the same tank.
LGP-INCIDENT-019.source_span: No cross-reference made to Finding 1.2 in the 2019 DISH audit (FT-11 flow sensor anomaly on same tank) — these two separate anomaly records on the same critical asset were never connected.

VSP-MAINT-012.content: The oven crown temperature in Oven #51 was 40°C below the setpoint due to a clogged regenerator.
VSP-MAINT-012.source_span: Oven #51:  Temperature deviation noted — oven crown temperature 40°C below           setpoint. Cause: clogged regenerator.

VSP-MAINT-012.content: The clogged regenerator in Oven #51 was cleaned to recover temperature.
The coal blend ratio was shifted to 40% COALSUPPLY-B, exceeding the standard 25%.
VSP-MAINT-012.source_span: Current blend ratio shifted to 40% COALSUPPLY-B (cheaper import coal) vs           standard 25%.

VSP-MAINT-012.content: COALSUPPLY-B has an ash content of 9.2%, which is higher than the 8.0% specification.
VSP-MAINT-012.source_span: Ash content of COALSUPPLY-B higher than spec (9.2% vs 8.0% spec).

VSP-MAINT-012.content: The use of COALSUPPLY-B is expected to result in higher sulphur and lower CSR in the coke.
VSP-MAINT-012.source_span: Impact on coke quality: expect slightly higher sulphur and lower CSR (Coke Strength after Reaction).

VSP-MAINT-012.content: No formal cross-divisional alert was issued to SMS-1 regarding the increased sulphur risk.
VSP-MAINT-012.source_span: No formal cross-divisional alert issued to SMS-1.

VSP-OEM-013.content: Argon purging duration should be between 15 and 25 minutes depending on the grade.
The argon flow rate must be set between 20 and 35 NL/min using the FCV-APS controller.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: Flow rate: 20–35 NL/min (set via FCV-APS controller)

VSP-OEM-013.content: The lance insertion depth must be 800mm ± 50mm from the ladle base.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: Lance insertion depth: 800mm ± 50mm from ladle base

VSP-OEM-013.content: Failure to eliminate gas pockets creates a risk of sudden volumetric gas expansion during mechanical agitation.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: Failure to eliminate such pockets creates risk of sudden volumetric gas expansion if mechanical agitation (e.g., ladle rotation, tilt, or slide gate opening) disturbs the pocket at a later stage.

VSP-OEM-013.content: A Type-3 explosion event involves rapid gas pocket expansion during ladle rotation or slide-gate activation, risking ladle tipover and hot metal spill.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: Failure to observe this precaution risks a Type-3 explosion event: rapid gas pocket expansion during ladle rotation or slide-gate activation at the casting platform, with risk of ladle tipover and catastrophic hot metal spill.

VSP-OEM-013.content: The FCV controller is identified as a component of the argon purge system that may be found defective.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: If the argon purge system (lance, FCV controller, or gas supply) is found defective during a heat sequence

VSP-OEM-013.content: Defective argon purge systems must be escalated to the SMS Superintendent immediately.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: the defect MUST be escalated to the SMS Superintendent immediately.

VSP-OEM-013.content: Processing heat without purging is only permitted with written Superintendent sign-off for a single heat.
VSP-OEM-013.source_span: Heat processing without purging is permitted ONLY with written Superintendent sign-off and only for a single heat.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Permit PTW-SMS1-2025-0204 was issued for refractory lining inspection and partial patch repair on ladle LD-003.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span:
PERMIT NUMBER: PTW-SMS1-2025-0204
DATE ISSUED: 2025-03-10
VALID UNTIL: 2025-03-12
WORK DESCRIPTION: Refractory lining inspection and partial patch repair,
                   ladle LD-003 (LF-3 service ladle).

VSP-PTW-014.content: The ladle must be cooled to an ambient temperature of 50°C or less before entry.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Cool ladle to ambient and confirm temperature ≤50°C before entry.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Zone A (ladle base) thickness was measured at 96mm, which is within spec but borderline.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Zone A (ladle base, bottom lining): thickness 96mm — within spec, borderline.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Zone B (ladle barrel) thickness was measured at 88mm, which is below the standard limit.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Zone B (ladle barrel, 1/3 height): thickness 88mm — below standard limit.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Zone B was patched using Refcoat-X700 high-alumina castable.
Zone C (ladle barrel, upper taper) thickness was measured at 104mm.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Zone C (ladle barrel, upper taper): thickness 104mm — OK.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Wear was noted at the slide gate seat ring.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Slide gate seat area: wear noted at seat ring.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Full relining of Zone A is deferred until 2025-04-10.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Schedule full relining within 4 weeks (i.e., by 2025-04-10).

VSP-PTW-014.content: Slide gate seat ring replacement was deferred because parts are in procurement.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span: Slide gate seat ring: Not replaced. Parts in procurement since 2025-03-14.

VSP-PTW-014.content: Work Order WO-SMS1-2025-0227 for Zone A relining is open and not started due to material backorder.
VSP-PTW-014.source_span:
Zone A relining: Work Order WO-SMS1-2025-0227 raised.
  Status as of 2025-06-01: OPEN / NOT STARTED — material on backorder.

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: APS-3 status is still faulty with an open work order for 4 weeks.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: APS-3 status: STILL FAULTY (WO open for 4 weeks)

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Purge was skipped on heat #1242 due to APS-3 fault.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: Morning shift log noted "purge skipped again on heat #1242."

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: No formal risk review was completed for the accumulated skips caused by the APS-3 fault.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: No formal risk review completed for accumulated skips.

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Ladle LF-3 was tapped from EAF at 1,618°C with a weight of 148 tonnes.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: Ladle LF-3 tapped from EAF at 1,618°C, 148 tonnes.

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Argon purge was skipped for heat #1244 due to APS-3 fault without superintendent sign-off or OEM 7.4.3 holding check.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: Argon purge: SKIPPED (APS-3 fault, no superintendent sign-off, no 15-minute holding check per OEM 7.4.3

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: A sudden explosion occurred during ladle rotation, causing ladle LD-003 to tip and spill 148 tonnes of liquid steel.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: During rotation, SUDDEN EXPLOSION. "Like a cannon." — Witness W. Kumar. Ladle LD-003 tipped. Approximately 148 tonnes of liquid steel at ~1,600°C poured onto shop floor.

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Crane CRANE-07 reported simultaneous cable/brake failure during the explosion.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: Crane CRANE-07 reported cable/brake failure simultaneously (under investigation — causal vs consequential).

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Eight persons were confirmed caught in the spill radius during the incident.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: 8 persons confirmed caught in spill radius.

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Plant emergency alarm was manually activated by W. Kumar.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: 16:15 — Plant emergency alarm activated (by W. Kumar, manually).

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Ladle LD-003 Zone A lining has been borderline since March.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: Ladle LD-003 Zone A lining borderline since March (see VSP-PTW-014)

VSP-SHIFT-015.content: Maintenance on APS-3 has been deferred with an open work order for over 4 weeks.
VSP-SHIFT-015.source_span: APS-3 fault open for 4+ weeks (see VSP-MAINT-009, VSP-SHIFT-010)

VSP-WO-018.content: Work order WO-SMS1-2025-0331 was raised for the Argon Purging Station APS-3 Flow Control Valve FCV-APS3.
VSP-WO-018.source_span:
WORK ORDER NUMBER: WO-SMS1-2025-0331
ASSET: Argon Purging Station APS-3, Flow Control Valve FCV-APS3

VSP-WO-018.content: FCV-APS3 is providing erratic output with flow fluctuating between 18–32 NL/min against a 25 NL/min setpoint.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: FCV-APS3 (argon flow control valve, APS-3 unit) giving erratic output. Setpoint 25 NL/min; actual flow fluctuating 18–32 NL/min and occasionally dropping to zero without alarm.

VSP-WO-018.content: The flow control valve deviation is 3–4 times the OEM specified tolerance of ±2 NL/min.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: OEM specifies ±2 NL/min control tolerance; current deviation 3–4×.

VSP-WO-018.content: Multiple heats were processed without purging due to the valve fault.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: Multiple heats processed without purge as a result (see shift logs).

VSP-WO-018.content: A spare actuator for the SteelTec component FCV-APS3 was ordered via PO-IEM-2025-0228.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: Spare actuator not in stores. Placed order with regional vendor (Order PO-IEM-2025-0228).

VSP-WO-018.content: Maintenance on FCV-APS3 was deferred and priority downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM because production was not stopped.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: TARGET DATE MISSED. Vendor delivery: "2–3 weeks." Priority downgraded to MEDIUM by maintenance planner (reason: "production not stopped").

VSP-WO-018.content: A partial fix was attempted by cleaning the actuator position sensor, but the fault recurred the same day.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: Partial fix attempted — actuator position sensor cleaned, temporary improvement observed (one partial-purge heat achieved at 8 minutes). Fault recurred same day.

VSP-WO-018.content: An explosion occurred at Caster-1, leading to the closure of the work order for investigation.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: 2025-06-06: EXPLOSION EVENT at Caster-1. WO closed as part of post-incident investigation freeze.

VSP-WO-018.content: The failure to escalate the safety-critical purging function maintenance indicates a systemic failure in the prioritization process.
VSP-WO-018.source_span: The priority downgrade from HIGH to MEDIUM on the same day as target date missed indicates a systemic failure of the maintenance prioritisation process.

VSP-020.content: The last inspection for the Argon Purging System was completed on 10 Jun 2025.
The next scheduled maintenance for the Argon Purging System is 10 Dec 2025.
Calibration for V-102 was deferred from 01 May 2025.
VSP-020.source_span: Deferred Work: V-102 calibration (deferred from 01 May 2025)

VSP-020.content: The pressure relief valve V-102 opens when pressure exceeds 2.5 bar.
VSP-020.source_span: V-102: Pressure Relief - Opens at >2.5 bar

VSP-020.content: A high pressure alarm is triggered at 3.0 bar via sensor P-101.
VSP-020.source_span: P-101: High Pressure Alarm - Triggers at 3.0 bar

VSP-020.content: A high temperature alarm is triggered at 500°C via sensor T-101.
VSP-020.source_span: T-101: High Temperature Alarm - Triggers at 500°C
